mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 20-day forward returns following a 0.3pp UNRATE increase are positive in more than 50% of observed instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicate a synchronized contraction in household spending and mortgage-driven wealth effects, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The signal fails if the joint decline of HOUST and RSAFS does not produce a statistically significant negative mean 60-day forward return compared to periods where at least one series stabilizes or rises.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production falls while consumer prices rise creates a stagflationary environment that erodes real corporate margins and forces equity repricing, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1.0% month-over-month while CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2% month-over-month
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered stagflation episodes over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, driving positive forward equity returns over the subsequent week as analysts revise profit forecasts upward.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 5-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 revisions show no directional bias (median return within ±0.5% of zero) across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, the cost of capital becomes sufficiently high to trigger credit tightening and equity valuation compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of trading days where FEDFUNDS stays above 5.0% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor demand environment, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails if the combination of rising PAYEMS and non-rising UNRATE does not yield a positive median 5-day forward return exceeding 0.2% over three consecutive decades.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter contraction in real GDP growth signals an impending recession, which systematically reduces corporate revenue expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if 20-day forward returns following a negative quarterly GDPC1 print are positive in more than 50% of instances over a 20-year rolling window.
